---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# genproc

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

`genproc` turns one-off iterative R procedures (`for`, `lapply()`,
`purrr::pmap()`, ...) into production-grade workflows by wrapping them
with orthogonal, composable execution layers. The goal is to make the
transition from *"a script that works on one case"* to *"a system that
runs reliably on many cases"* an architectural step, not an improvised
rewrite.

Two layers are always active:

- **Logged** — each case produces a structured log row with the real
  traceback (captured via `withCallingHandlers()`) and timing.
- **Reproducibility** — each run records the R version, loaded package
  versions, execution environment, the exact mask, and hashes of the
  file inputs referenced by the mask. Silent non-reproducibility
  between runs (e.g. an input file changed without its path changing)
  is flagged to the user.

Further layers are planned and will be composable with the defaults:

- parallel execution
- non-blocking (background) execution
- monitored progress
- error replay

`genproc` has **zero Shiny dependency**. A companion package
(`genprocShiny`) will later build a UI on top of these functions.

## Status

Early development. The public API is not stable yet. The package is
not on CRAN.

## Installation

Install the development version from GitHub:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("danielrak/genproc")
```

## Roadmap (v0.1)

- `from_example_to_function()`: turn an example expression into a
  parameterized function (AST walker, rewritten modularly so a single
  human can maintain it without AI assistance).
- `from_function_to_mask()`: derive an iteration mask from a function
  signature.
- `genproc()`: run a function over a mask with the mandatory *logged*
  and *reproducibility* layers.
- Stable `case_id` injection, structured log, reproducibility report.

## License

MIT. See [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md).
